fieldValues.value: containerId filter uses RFC-009 I-66 three-condition membership; instance file and relations file are authoritative

A containerId filter MUST use the three-condition membership definition of RFC-009 I-66: an instance is a member of a container if (1) its instanceId appears in Container.rootInstanceIds[], OR (2) its instanceId appears in Container.memberInstanceIds[], OR (3) it is reachable via transitive contains Relation traversal from any instance in Container.rootInstanceIds[]. The authoritative source for membership is the instance file and the relations file. An implementation MAY use the instanceIndex in manifest.json as a cache for performance, but MUST treat the instance file and relations file as authoritative when they differ from the cache.

Reusing I-66's predicate ensures that containerId filter (forward: given a container, which instances?) and containers_for_instance reverse lookup (given an instance, which containers?) are exact inverses. Using a different definition would break this symmetry. The instanceIndex cache permission acknowledges that iterating relations for every query may be costly in large repositories.
